entries.definition:
w–d–d love, affection, friendship; to desire, to wish for; to be
amicable. Of this root, six forms occur 29 times in the Qur’an: وَدَّ
wadda 16 times; يُوَادُّون yuwāddūn once; وُدّ wudd once; وَدُود wadūd
twice; مَوَدَّة mawaddatun eight times and وَدّ wadd once.

وَدَّ wadda a [v. trans.] to desire, to wish for (2:266) أَيَوَدُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ
تَكُونَ لَهُ جَنَّةٌ مِنْ نَخِيلٍ وَأَعْنَابٍ would any of you like to have a garden of
palm trees and vines?

1017
